assertions.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else
assertions.evidence: Step 1.1 of Phase 1 Startup Sequence: 'Tool: Read on _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml' — this is the very first action in the plan.

assertions.text: Skips story 1.1 which is already done
assertions.evidence: Under 'Resume Logic' in Key Decisions: 'Story 1.1 is done -> skipped entirely.' Step 1.4 confirms Story 1.1 status is 'done' and the work target is Story 1.2.

assertions.text: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
assertions.evidence: Story 1.2 section states: 'ready-for-dev -> start at Step 3 (Develop Story). Steps 1 (Create Story) and 2 (Validate Story) are skipped because the story file already exists and was validated.'

assertions.text: Calls TeamCreate before spawning sub-agents
assertions.evidence: Phase 2 'Create Team' explicitly calls 'TeamCreate with team_name: "bmad-auto"' with the note 'Done once before entering the main loop.' Phase 3 (sub-agent spawning) comes after Phase 2.

assertions.text: Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent
assertions.evidence: Step 3 of Story 1.2: 'Agent with name: "story-developer", team_name: "bmad-auto"'. All subsequent sub-agents also use team_name: "bmad-auto".

assertions.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else
assertions.evidence: Step 1 under Phase 1: 'Read on _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml' — listed as the very first action.

assertions.text: Skips story 1.1 which is already done
assertions.evidence: Critical Decisions section: 'Skip story 1.1 - It is already done, so no action needed.'

assertions.text: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
assertions.evidence: Key Decision Point section: 'Since story 1.2 is ready-for-dev, this means the story file has already been created and validated. I would skip story creation and validation, and start directly at the development step.'

assertions.text: Calls TeamCreate before spawning sub-agents
assertions.evidence: The plan explicitly admits: 'No TeamCreate call - I have no knowledge of the Agent team architecture, so I would not call TeamCreate before spawning sub-agents.'

assertions.text: Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent
assertions.evidence: The plan explicitly admits: 'No team-based sub-agents - I would not use the Agent tool with team_name parameter. I might use disposable sub-agents or just do everything myself.'

assertions.text: Checks if sprint-status.yaml exists before proceeding
assertions.evidence: Step 1: 'Check if _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml exists.' This is the very first action, per the SKILL.md Startup Sequence.

assertions.text: Invokes bmad-help skill when sprint-status.yaml is missing
assertions.evidence: Step 2: 'Invoke skill: "bmad-help"' and 'Report the suggestions returned by bmad-help to the user.'

assertions.text: Does NOT create a team or spawn sub-agents
assertions.evidence: Step 3 titled 'There Is No Step 3': 'The orchestrator halts after step 2. No team is created, no sub-agents are spawned, no stories are processed.'

assertions.text: Checks if sprint-status.yaml exists before proceeding
assertions.evidence: Step 2 under 'Look for Implementation Tracking': 'Check if there is a sprint-status.yaml or equivalent. Finding: No sprint-status.yaml exists.'

assertions.text: Invokes bmad-help skill when sprint-status.yaml is missing
assertions.evidence: The plan explicitly states: 'That I should invoke a bmad-help skill to guide the user to the correct next step.' — listed under 'What I Do NOT Know'.

assertions.text: Does NOT create a team or spawn sub-agents
assertions.evidence: Step 4 'Likely (Incorrect) Actions' states: 'Create a sprint-status tracking file myself' and 'Begin creating story files and implementing code directly.'

assertions.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml for current state
assertions.evidence: Step 1: 'Read the sprint status file at: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml.' The plan identifies this as the first and primary action.

assertions.text: Provides a summary of epic and story progress
assertions.evidence: Step 2 'Summarize Status' lists: '1. Overall progress: Count total stories vs. stories with status done. 2. Current epic. 3. Per-story breakdown. 4. Blockers/issues.'

assertions.text: Does NOT enter the main loop or spawn any sub-agents
assertions.evidence: Step 4: 'Per the Status Query section: "Do NOT continue the main loop -- just report and wait."'

assertions.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml for current state
assertions.evidence: Step 1 searches for status files including '**/sprint-status*'. Step 2: 'If a status tracking file is found, read it and parse the structure.'

assertions.text: Provides a summary of epic and story progress
assertions.evidence: Step 4: 'Compile the findings into a status summary: Total stories and how many are done / in-progress / remaining, Which epic is currently active, Any blockers or issues.'

assertions.text: Does NOT enter the main loop or spawn any sub-agents
assertions.evidence: The plan describes a purely read-and-report workflow with no implementation actions. Passes by default since without the skill there is no orchestration architecture.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies that all epics and stories are done
assertions.evidence: Step 2 parses sprint-status.yaml and confirms: 'All epics have status done. All stories within every epic have status done. Branch taken: If all finished path.'

assertions.text: Invokes bmad-help skill to suggest next actions
assertions.evidence: Step 3: 'Invoke the Skill tool with skill: "bmad-help" to get suggestions for next actions.' Then: 'Relay the suggestions from bmad-help to the user.'

assertions.text: Does NOT create a team or start working on stories
assertions.evidence: 'What Does NOT Happen' section: 'No team creation - TeamCreate is only called before starting the main loop, and the main loop is never entered. No sub-agents spawned.'

assertions.text: Correctly identifies that all epics and stories are done
assertions.evidence: The plan states: 'Since the user states all epics and stories are already marked as done, I would confirm this by reading the file, then report that all implementation work is complete.'

assertions.text: Invokes bmad-help skill to suggest next actions
assertions.evidence: The plan explicitly states: 'Without the bmad-auto SKILL.md guidance, I would NOT know to invoke the bmad-help skill to suggest next actions.' Instead offers generic suggestions.

assertions.text: Does NOT create a team or start working on stories
assertions.evidence: Under 'What I would NOT do': 'I would NOT create a team or spawn sub-agents. I would NOT attempt to start working on any stories.'

assertions.text: Sub-agent or orchestrator investigates if the manual task can be automated (AWS CLI, IaC tools)
assertions.evidence: Two-layer investigation. Sub-agent investigates automation first including 'AWS CLI, CloudFormation/CDK, Terraform, IAM policy creation.' The orchestrator then reviews: 'If the orchestrator sees an automation approach the sub-agent missed, send instructions to try it.'

assertions.text: Does NOT immediately halt and ask the user - tries automation first
assertions.evidence: Step 3 decision point shows automation attempted BEFORE user escalation. Only Step 4 halts for user: 'If truly manual, halt and ask the user.' Cites Critical Rule 14: 'Automate before asking for help.'

assertions.text: Sub-agent or orchestrator investigates if the manual task can be automated (AWS CLI, IaC tools)
assertions.evidence: The plan explicitly states: 'I would not attempt to automate the AWS resource creation myself. I would not investigate whether the manual task could actually be scripted or automated.'

assertions.text: Does NOT immediately halt and ask the user - tries automation first
assertions.evidence: Step 1 is: 'Halt and ask the user for clarification.' The agent immediately stops to ask about AWS account, region, bucket name, IAM permissions.

notes: with_skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 16 assertions and all 5 evals.
without_skill passes only 9/16 assertions (56.25%) and 1/5 evals (status-query, which is non-discriminating).
The strongest discriminators are manual-task (2/2 vs 0/2) and missing-sprint-status (3/3 vs 1/3).
status-query is the weakest eval -- both configurations pass all assertions. The without_skill run passes trivially on the 'no sub-agents' assertion since it has no orchestration architecture.
Key skill-unique behaviors: TeamCreate/team_name agent architecture, bmad-help fallback invocation, automation-first manual task handling, sprint-status.yaml as a hard gate.
Comparison summary confirms with_skill wins 5/5 evals (mapped through blinded A/B labels).
